trajectory.behavior: The payload is a loader or dropper that communicates with two IP addresses over HTTPS, and performs DNS lookups for two domains. It creates a process in the temporary directory and loads several notable modules, including some related to Windows API and networking. The payload contains anti-VM strings, suggesting that it is designed to evade detection by virtual machine software.
trajectory.ttp: T1071 - Process Injection, T1047 - Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) (for communication with the outside world), T1568 - Dynamic Resolution (for DNS lookups), T1218 - Signed Binary Proxy Execution (for loading notable modules), T1497 - Virtualization/Sandbox Evasion (for anti-VM strings)
